data.title: Materion beat Q4 estimated earnings by 3.33% with EPS of $1.55, revenue up $52.88M YoY. Last quarter EPS miss led to 0.53% share price drop.
data.description: Materion, listed on the NYSE under the symbol MTRN, disclosed its Q4 financial results on February 12, 2026, at 06:47 AM. The company exceeded expected earnings by 3.33%, with an EPS of $1.55 compared to the estimated $1.5. Revenue saw a $52.88 million increase from the previous year. In the previous quarter, Materion fell short on EPS by $0.0, leading to a 0.53% decrease in share price. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Kimco Realty reported Q4 2025 net income of $143.6M, FFO of $294.3M, and full-year net income of $554.4M, FFO of $1.2B. Occupancy reached all-time highs, shares repurchased and new executive appointments announced. Dividend of $0.26 per share declared. Outlook for 2026: Net income $0.80-$0.84, FFO $1.80-$1.84. More: https://finance.yahoo.com/news/kimco-realty-announces-fourth-quarter-113000479.html.
data.description: Kimco Realty, a real estate investment trust, reported results for Q4 2025 and full year 2025 with net income of $0.21 per diluted share for Q4 2025 and $0.82 per diluted share for full year 2025. Funds from operations per diluted share increased to $0.44 for Q4 and $1.76 for the full year. The company achieved a 4.8% growth in FFO in Q4 and a 6.7% increase for the full year. Pro-rata portfolio occupancy was at an all-time high of 96.4%. Kimco repurchased 3.1 million shares of common stock. The 2026 outlook includes a net income of $0.80 to $0.84 per diluted share and FFO of $1.80 to $1.84 per diluted share. Important numbers include $74.0 million for acquiring The Shoppes at 82nd Street, 435 leases totaling 2.7 million square feet signed in Q4, and a total of 565 U.S. shopping centers and mixed-use assets as of December 31, 2025, totaling 100 million square feet of gross leasable space.

data.title: US Foods Q4 2025: Total case volume up 0.8%, net sales $9.8B, net income up 178.8% to $184M, Adjusted EBITDA up 11.1%.
data.description: US Foods Holding Corp. shared their financial performance for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2025. During the fourth quarter, total case volume saw a 0.8% increase, with independent restaurant case volume up by 4.1%. Net sales rose by 3.3% to $9.8 billion, while gross profit saw a 3.2% increase to $1.7 billion. Net income experienced a significant surge, rising by 178.8% to $184 million. Additionally, adjusted EBITDA increased by 11.1% to $4 billion.

data.title: Iron Mountain (NYSE: IRM) posts record Q4 results in 2025, marking 5th straight year of all-time high revenue and EBITDA.
data.description: Iron Mountain Incorporated, a leading provider of information management services, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025. The company, listed on NYSE as IRM, reported a record-breaking performance in Q4, exceeding expectations and marking the fifth consecutive year of reaching all-time highs in Revenue, Adjusted EBITDA, and AFFO. The team's dedication to providing innovative solutions to customers has contributed to this success.

data.title: Core Natural Resources reports strong Q4 2025 results with $107.3 million in operating cash and $27.0 million in free cash flow.
data.description: The mining operation at Leer South has resumed longwall activities and is on track to achieve targeted production rates for the year 2026. Similarly, the transition to a new seam at West Elk has been successfully completed, with operations also at expected production rates for 2026. The company has reported a strong financial performance, with a net cash flow from operating activities amounting to $107.3 million and free cash flow reaching $27.0 million.
STATEMENT FROM SIR JIM RATCLIFFE IN RESPONSE TO UK PRESS COVERAGE

data.title: Granite (NYSE: GVA) reports Q4 2025 net income of $52M ($1.03 per share) compared to $41M last year. Adjusted net income was $65M ($1.40 per share) compared to $56M last year.
data.description: Granite (NYSE: GVA) of Watsonville, Calif, revealed its financial results for the quarter and year ending December 31, 2025. In the fourth quarter of 2025, the company reported a net income of $52 million, equivalent to $1.03 per diluted share. This marked an increase from the net income of $41 million, or $0.84 per diluted share, during the same period the previous year. Additionally, adjusted net income for the fourth quarter was $65 million, or $1.40 per diluted share, compared to $56 million, or $1.23 per diluted share, in the preceding year.
RATCLIFFE: "I AM SORRY THAT MY CHOICE OF LANGUAGE HAS OFFENDED SOME PEOPLE IN THE UK AND EUROPE"

data.title: Darren Entwistle to retire June 30 after 26 years; Victor Dodig named next TELUS President. Successful succession planning.
data.description: Victor Dodig, a skilled and accomplished business leader, has been appointed as a result of a thorough succession planning process conducted by the Board of Directors at TELUS. Darren Entwistle, credited for steering TELUS' transformation from a regional telecom company to a prominent global technology innovator, has led the growth of the company. The announcement was made on February 12th in Vancouver, British Columbia.

data.title: Brookfield Corporation announces record distributable earnings of $5.4 billion, quarterly dividend up by 17% and over $1 billion shares repurchased in 2025.
data.description: Brookfield Corporation reported strong financial results for the year ended December 31, 2025, with a net income of $3.2 billion and distributable earnings of $6.0 billion. The company repurchased over $1 billion of its shares and raised its quarterly dividend by 17%. Asset Management recorded $112 billion of inflows, while Wealth Solutions saw distributable earnings grow by 24%. Operating Businesses generated cash flows, with DE of $1.6 billion for the year. The company completed a record $91 billion of monetizations in 2025 and ended the year with a record $188 billion of capital available for deployment. Brookfield's balance sheet remains strong, with assets totaling $518.97 billion and equity of $166.19 billion. The company also returned $1.6 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.

data.title: Onity Group announced full year and Q4 2025 results: record net income of $185M & $126M; EPS of $21.46 & $14.24 respectively; $1.1B in total revenue; $85B in servicing additions; $10M share repurchase program established.
data.description: Onity Group Inc. reported record net income and earnings per share for the Full Year 2025, with $185 million in net income and $21.46 in diluted EPS, as well as a return on equity of 35%. Additionally, they achieved a book value per share of $74 and saw profitable growth with total GAAP revenue increasing 9% to $1.1 billion compared to FY 2024. In the Fourth Quarter 2025, the company reported a net income of $126 million and diluted EPS of $14.24, with a return on equity of 89%. They also announced a $10 million share repurchase program. The 2026 outlook includes an adjusted ROE guidance range of 13% – 15% and servicing UPB growth of 5% – 15%. Onity's Chair, President, and CEO, Glen Messina, expressed confidence in the company's continued growth and value creation. The company aims to simplify its business, invest in innovation, and use new capital to support growth and deleveraging.

data.title: Scorpio Tankers Inc. reported $128.1 million net income for Q4 2025, with a $0.45/share dividend declared. Revenue increased due to higher TCE rates propelled by strong demand. Financials show deleveraging efforts and strategic vessel sales. Company has plans for newbuilding vessels. Cash on hand is $937.1 million.
data.description: Scorpio Tankers Inc. reported its financial results for the three months ended December 31, 2025. The company had a net income of $128.1 million for the quarter, with adjusted net income of $80.0 million. This represents basic and diluted earnings per share of $2.72 and $2.59 respectively. For the year ended December 31, 2025, the company reported a net income of $344.3 million and adjusted net income of $269.5 million. The board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.45 per common share, with a payment date of March 20, 2026. As of February 12, 2026, there were 51,762,790 common shares issued and outstanding. The company also disclosed recent significant events including vessel purchases, sales, and financial activities. Scorpio Tankers had $937.1 million in unrestricted cash and cash equivalents as of February 10, 2026, with debt balances outlined for various credit facilities. The company's fourth quarter financial results saw increased TCE revenue and a rise in average daily TCE rates due to market factors, despite lower operating costs and depreciation expenses. Total operating income for the quarter was $133.1 million. This information was shared as part of their financial report for the quarter.

data.title: TELUS declares a quarterly dividend of $0.4184 per share payable on April 1, 2026 to shareholders of record on March.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia on February 12, 2026, it was announced that the Board of Directors has approved a quarterly dividend of $0.4184 CAD per share for the Common shares. Shareholders of record by the close of business on March 1, 2026 will be eligible to receive this dividend, which is set to be paid out on April 1, 2026.

data.title: TELUS reports strong Q4 and full-year 2025 results with 377,000 total customer growth, including 50,000 mobile phone additions.
data.description: In the fourth quarter, a prominent player in the industry experienced significant growth with a total of 377,000 new Mobile and Fixed customers. This comprised 50,000 mobile phone users, 287,000 connected devices, and 35,000 internet additions. The increase was attributed to the sustained popularity of their premium bundled services across the country. Moreover, they reported positive revenue from their mobile network operations during this period.
